page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.medium is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores, running at 1 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, meaning storage requirements must be met through external network-attached block storage.

With dedicated CPU allocation and a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.m1.medium offers predictable compute performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core or burstable instances. This resource profile makes it a cost-efficient option for standard enterprise workloads that require stable CPU performance but do not demand specialized hardware accelerators or high-speed local NVMe drives. The instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, including medium-traffic web servers, application hosting, microservices, and development or testing environments.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.medium is a general-purpose virtual machine instance built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with a single thread per core, paired with 16.0 GB of system memory. This configuration establishes a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on external block storage for data persistence. With dedicated CPU allocation, it provides stable and predictable performance, avoiding the resource sharing of burstable tiers. The ecs.m1.medium is designed for standard enterprise workloads, making it a reliable and cost-efficient choice for hosting application servers, web servers, microservices, and development environments that require consistent compute resources without specialized hardware demands.

tagline: A balanced virtual machine offering dedicated compute cores and system memory for predictable general-purpose workloads.
